rulings.Case Title: VATCHE CABAYAN M.D. VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Pltf'S Motion To Compel Further Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday Feb. 21, 2012 Line 2 - PLAINTIFF VATCHE CABAYAN M.D., VATCHE CABAYAN MEDICAL CORPORATION A CALIFORNIA (Amended) Motion To Compel Further Responses. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Parties to address the following issues at the hearing:(1) Elements of Ins. Code §1877.4 "public interest" hearing as concerns the facts of the instant case. The court does not find the opposing declarations of Darrel Woo and Matthew Beltramo instructive as to the court's potential findings. (2) With respect to the named cross-defendants, whether the privilege attaches to materials which have been seized pursuant to a search warrant or to the identities of any persons providing affidavits in support of the issuance of a search warrant. 3) Whether Cross-complainant's RICO claim creates a waiver of the asserted privilege under Ins. §1877.4 and the scope of such a waiver.(4) Relevance of special interrogatories numbers 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7,8, 9, 10, and 12. (5) Whether a protective order or sealing order pursuant to CRC 2.550, et seq., is appropriate and the terms (and required findings) of any such order(s).Parties are also instructed to meet and confer prior to the hearing on the following: 1) Terms and scope of any suitable protective order or sealing order if the court grants the motion in whole or in part; 2) in view of court's questions, the parties should also discuss a possible stipulated disposition.Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DHILLON & SMITH, LLP VS. BRIAN HURLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing for Tuesday, February 21, 2012, Line 4, CROSS DEFENDANT DHILLON & SMITH, LLP, HAROLD SMITH, HARMEET DHILLON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Dhillon's Request for Judicial Notice Unopposed and granted. Complaint Motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication denied. 1. Breach of contract. Based on the putative undisputed material fact (#1) that the written agreement was not modified. Admissible evidence shows it was modified. Hurley Dec. 18 2. Account stated. Based on the putative undisputed material fact (#8) that Dhillon performed all its obligations. Admissible evidence shows not all obligations were performed. Hurley Dec 20. 3. Foreclosure of Attorney Lien. Based on the putative undisputed material fact (#14) that Dhillon performed all its obligations. Admissible evidence shows not all obligations were performed. Hurley Dec 20. 4. Fraud. Based on the putative undisputed material fact (#19) that defendants promised plaintiffs they would pay for services regardless of outcome. Admissible evidence shows the parties' understanding was modified. Hurley Dec. 18. Based on the putative undisputed material fact (#27) that defendants concealed their financial situation. Admissible evidence shows the defendants did not conceal their financial situation. Hurley Dec. 18. Cross-complaints Motion for summary judgment denied. Motions for summary adjudication granted as indicated below " 1: Breach of Fiduciary Duty. DENY o Proof of breach. Based on the putative undisputed material fact (#35) that defendants agreed there was conflict and all interests were aligned. Admissible evidence shows the parties' understanding was not made in writing and accordingly ineffective, or that the defendants' did not knowingly waive conflicts. Hurley Dec 28. Based on the putative undisputed material fact (#38) that plaintiffs advised defendants the budget would be exceeded. Admissible evidence shows the plaintiffs did not so advise defendants. Hurley Dec 9. o Proof of damages. No evidence of damages consequent on the alleged breach has been shown. The evidence cited by defendants, Hurley Dec. 6-8, does not show or suggest damages. The fact that sums have not yet been distributed by the law firm does not establish damages, as the firm is entitled to hold back those sums until the underlying dispute is resolved. Rule 4-100(A)(2). " 2: Conversion. GRANT. o Proof of right to possession. Defendants address undisputed facts #49, 55, 56 by stating that they do not have evidence on the matter. This is equivalent to affirming that the fact is undisputed. Facts #51 and #52 are not disputed by the cited evidence (Hurley Dec. 31), which makes only conclusory statements and does not in fact contradict the putative undisputed fact. Thus there are no material facts in dispute. o Proof of money converted. No disputed facts. o Defendants provide no argument that the lien is invalid; an asserted violation of the professional rules of conduct does not establish the invalidity of a lien. " 3: Demand for Accounting. GRANT. o Accounting has been provided. Re: facts #57 & #58: see discussions of #51. Re: facts 60-61, see discussions of #49. Thus there are no material facts in dispute. " 4: Fraud. DENY. o Proof of false representation. Based on the putative undisputed material fact (#65) that plaintiffs advised defendants that budget would be exceeded. Admissible evidence shows the contrary. Hurley Dec. 9. Based on the putative undisputed material fact (#66) that plaintiffs advised defendants of realistic assessments of possible outcomes and estimates of strength of the case. Admissible evidence shows the contrary. Hurley Dec. 4 & Ex. F. Based on the putative undisputed material fact (#73) that plaintiffs did not modify the fee agreement. Admissible evidence shows the contrary. Hurley Dec. 18.

rulings.Case Title: DHILLON & SMITH, LLP VS. BRIAN HURLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Defendant Dhillon & Smith Llp, Harold Smith, Harmeet Dhillon'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Cross-Defendant Dhillon & Smith Llp, Harold Smith, Harmeet Dhillon'S Motion For Summary Judgment- part 2 - o Proof of reasonable reliance. The admissible evidence suggests that in reliance on the asserted fraud defendants incurred liability for fees. Ex. E. (January invoice). " 5: Professional negligence. GRANT o Statute of limitations. Moot in light of ruling below. o Proof of skill of reasonable attorney. The only dispute fact is #85, but the evidence in support offered by defendants is incompetent and inadmissible. Expert testimony is required on the matter and defendants have none. " 6: Negligent misrepresentation. DENY. o Proof of negligence. Based on the putative undisputed material fact (#93) that plaintiffs advised defendant that the original budget would be exceeded. Admissible evidence shows the contrary. Hurley Dec. 9. Based on the putative undisputed material fact (#94) that plaintiffs advised defendants of realistic assessments of possible outcomes and estimates of strength of the case. Admissible evidence shows the contrary. Hurley Dec. 4 & Ex. F. Other evidentiary rulings: Objections to the evidence relied on above are overruled. Objections to evidence not relied on above are sustained, on the basis of relevance. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/CK

rulings.Case Title: NORTHERN CALIFORNIA DISTRICT COUNCIL OF VS. DAVID ROSSI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award; Index Of Federal Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing for Tuesday, February 21, 2012, Line 9, PETITIONER NORTHERN CALIFORNIA DISTRICT COUNCIL OF Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration AwardPetition to Confirm Arbitration Award is unopposed by Respondents David Rossi, individually and doing business as Tucker Engineering. The Petition is granted with respect to Respondents David Rossi, individually and doing business as Tucker Engineering, and attorneys' fees are awarded in favor of Petitioner and against respondents David Rossi, individually and doing business as Tucker Engineering. The Petition is denied as to Respondent Sunstone Construction Inc {SCI}. SCI did not sign an agreement containing arbitration clause. Section 11 of the Master Agreement, cited in the Petition 7 filed December 15, 2011 does state that its terms apply to subcontractors but SCI is not a party to the Master Agreement. SCI notified Petitioner of this reasoning on the filing of the Petition, and SCI has unjustifiably incurred fees in the sum of $3,533 in responding to Petitioner's frivolous filings. However SCI has not provided the Court with authority either under federal law (the parties appear to agree federal law may apply) or state law (i.e. CCP § 128.7) authorizing the award of fees in favor of a respondent who successfully resists a petition to confirm. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
